title: Spicy Chewy Indulgent Algerian Taco Shells Stir-fry
description: This spicy, chewy, indulgent Algerian taco shell dish features hard taco shells cooked in a mediterranean-friendly stir-fry with olive oil and spices to create an irresistible flavor combo.

ingredients.name: Taco Shells
ingredients.amount: 6 (soft taco shells or hard taco shells, halved if needed)

instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add chili powder and ground cumin; cook for about 30 seconds.
Add minced garlic, oregano leaves, and red pepper flakes to the pan. Sauté until fragrant (about 2 minutes).
Fold in zucchini slices and diced bell peppers into the pan. Cook for another minute or two until vegetables are slightly softened.
Toss half of the taco shells with a bit of olive oil on each side, placing them shell-side down in the pan. Cook for about 3-4 minutes per side or until golden brown and crispy.
Remove cooked taco shells from the pan and set aside. Use the same pan to stir-fry remaining vegetables and taco shells.
Add crumbled Greek yogurt along with any cooking juices, stirring well to combine. Continue to cook for another minute to allow flavors to meld together.
Transfer the stir-fried taco shells into a serving dish and top with zucchini-bell pepper mixture and drizzled lemon zest. Serve hot as a meal.
